$comment: Canonical Nostr wire contract between ndisc (publisher) and the glmps web viewers (consumers). v2 supersedes v1 — see schema/README.md and schema/v2-proposal.md. Source of truth: xjmzx/ndisc src-tauri/src/lib.rs (release_event / delete_release); the contract test `mod schema_v2` pins ndisc's output to this file.

release.content: Release notes — plain text. May be an empty string.

d.note: The ONLY structurally guaranteed tag. The `disco-vault:` prefix is the legacy app name and is frozen — do not change it to `ndisc:`.

medium.note: OPTIONAL — frequently absent. Consumers must not require it.

condition.note: Discogs grade verbatim, e.g. 'Near Mint (NM or M-)'.

source.note: URL — emitted only when it is http(s).

genre.note: NEW IN v2. Up to three ordered genre slots: first = primary, second = secondary, third = tertiary. Each slug is one of the 22 values in `genreSlugs`. Order of `genre` tags in the emitted event IS the priority order — emitters MUST preserve it, consumers MUST treat it as authoritative. Invariants enforced by the emitter and validated by conformant consumers (see `genreInvariants`).

release.rule: Every tag except `d` is omitted entirely when its value is empty. Consumers MUST require only `d` structurally and treat all other tags — including `title`, `artist`, `medium`, and `genre` — as optional.

a.note: Addressable coordinate of the deleted release. Authoritative — match this verbatim.

k.note: Redundant: the kind is already encoded in `a`. Consumers may ignore `k`.

deletion.rule: Consumers MUST apply deletions client-side. Some relays (e.g. Primal) do not enforce kind:5, so server-side filtering cannot be relied on.

genreSlugs._comment: The complete set of valid `genre` tag values in v2. Slug expansions inside a v2-compatible scheme are permitted by emitter-and-reader convention (new optional slug added to this list) provided existing slugs remain stable. Renaming or removing a slug requires a v3 bump. Note: `mains` vs `electronicSubs` is a visual / palette grouping (sub-slugs share the magenta hue family for chip colour); semantically all 22 slugs are pure peers — see genreInvariants.
genreSlugs.compoundDisplayRule: Compound PAIR slugs (classical-folk, dnb-jungle, drone-noise, footwork-trap) join two distinct genres with a hyphen on the wire and SHOULD render with a slash in UI (e.g. dnb/jungle). EXCEPTION: hip-hop is a single genre name that contains a hyphen — it is NOT a pair and renders verbatim. Implementations MUST slash only the known pair slugs; a blind slug.replace(/-/g,'/') would mangle hip-hop into hip/hop. Reference: ndisc src/lib/genre.ts SLASH_DISPLAY_SLUGS.

genreInvariants._comment: Cross-slot constraints. The emitter enforces these at capture time; conformant consumers SHOULD validate on read with strict-but-recoverable handling (silently drop the offending slot, keep the rest). v2.1: all 22 slugs are pure peers — `electronic` + any electronic sub (e.g. `techno`) is permitted in the same event. Meaning composes by stacking slugs rather than implying parent semantics.
genreInvariants.distinct: All emitted `genre` slugs in a single event MUST be distinct.
genreInvariants.denseOrdering: Slots are dense on the wire — present slugs occupy the lowest indices. A consumer receiving a sparse sequence (e.g. via a non-canonical re-emitter) SHOULD compact before indexing.

validation.unknownGenreSlug: Treat as absent (no colour, no chip) OR render with a neutral fallback. Do NOT throw or fail the parse.
validation.invariantViolation: Silently drop the offending slot(s); keep the remaining valid ones. Do NOT throw.

profile.note: ndisc reads the author's kind:0 metadata (name / nip05 / picture) for display. It never publishes kind:0.

changePolicy: FROZEN once shipped. Additive slug additions inside `genreSlugs` (keeping existing slugs stable) are permitted by emitter-and-reader convention without forcing a v3 bump. Any other change to the emitted event format — renaming/removing slugs, changing tag semantics, breaking ordering — is a coordinated v3 bump: add release.v3.json and migrate consumers, never edit this file.
rolloutFromV1: v1 readers ignore the `genre` tag (additive rule) and surface no genre data until upgraded. ndisc emits only v2 format; no v1-compatible proxy (e.g. NIP-12 `t` tags) is added. See schema/v2-proposal-glmps-reply.md for the v2-only emission decision rationale.
